items.title: Finding a lost production model
items.scenario: On-call sees accuracy drop after a deploy. The deployment ticket lists run ID `a3f91c2` but not the hyperparameters.
items.explanation: You open the tracking UI, search by run ID `a3f91c2`, and retrieve learning rate, batch size, and the registered model version that was promoted—no need to re-guess from git history alone.

items.title: Hyperparameter sweep with tags
items.scenario: A team runs 20 learning-rate trials overnight under experiment `sentiment-lr-sweep`.
items.explanation: Each run gets a unique ID automatically. They tag all runs `lr-sweep` and the best one `candidate`. Monday standup filters by tag instead of scrolling 20 similar names.

items.title: Promoting through the registry
items.scenario: Validation passes for run `7b2e10` saving `model.pkl`. Policy requires Staging before Production.
items.explanation: They register `model.pkl` as `fraud-detector` version 5 linked to run `7b2e10`, move v5 to Staging, run integration tests, then transition the same version to Production—lineage stays tied to one ID.

items.title: Fair run comparison after a bug fix
items.scenario: A data preprocessing bug was fixed mid-week; older runs used the wrong normalization.
items.explanation: They tag post-fix runs `norm-v2` and compare only those tagged runs against each other, excluding pre-fix IDs so leaderboard rankings are not polluted.
